question.text: An AI-only scheme of an AIF may extend its tenure by a maximum of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An AI-only scheme may extend its tenure by maximum 5 years (inclusive of tenure already extended, if any, prior to conversion). This is more generous than the standard 2-year extension for regular AIF schemes. **The Trap:** The 5-year limit is inclusive of any prior extensions — so if a regular fund extended by 1 year before converting to AI-only, only 4 more years of extension remain. **Cross-Exam Context:** Differential treatment for AI-only schemes is a recent regulatory development tested in current XIX-A curriculum. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The extended tenure flexibility is a significant advantage of AI-only schemes. For long-gestation investments (infrastructure, real estate), the ability to extend up to 5 years provides more time for value realization, potentially improving returns.
